demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents, fully dependent on them for all daily care

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: This is her mum calling. Our daughter has Angelman syndrome and she's non-verbal. For the last 6 hours she's been laughing uncontrollably and flapping her hands much more than usual, really excitable and agitated, and she's had two spells where she went blank and stared while her right arm jerked for about half a minute, then went floppy and very sleepy afterwards. Her walking is a lot more wobbly than normal and she keeps bumping into the furniture. We missed her evening seizure medicine last night.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The blank-staring jerking spells and the extra agitation have never been this bad in one day, they keep happening, and we're worried she'll hurt herself falling or that it will get worse.

symptom_narrative.onset: started building up over the last 6 hours today
symptom_narrative.duration: about 6 hours and still happening on and off
symptom_narrative.character: much more hand-flapping and unprovoked laughing than her usual, very excitable and agitated, plus two separate spells of going blank and staring with her right arm jerking rhythmically for around 30 seconds each, followed by going floppy and drowsy
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst her behaviour and unsteadiness have been in a single day; the staring-and-jerking spells are new in how frequent they are
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the jerking was in her right arm; the agitation and unsteadiness are all over
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: comes in episodes with more settled but very drowsy periods in between, and between spells she goes back to her usual excitable self
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: she seems to settle and recover to her normal self in between the spells
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: nothing the parents can identify; it has just kept happening through the day
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: very unsteady wide-based walking with bumping into furniture; drowsiness and floppiness after each spell
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: she has a known seizure tendency as part of her syndrome and is on a daily preventive medicine; the parents have not seen this many spells in one day before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: fully dependent on her parents for everything; the unsteadiness and agitation are making it hard to keep her safe at home

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: Angelman syndrome, diagnosed in early childhood
past_medical_history.how_managed: under long-term specialist and developmental follow-up

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: severe global developmental delay and is completely non-verbal
a seizure tendency that is part of her syndrome, normally kept under control with a daily preventive medicine

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: sodium valproate, taken every day to prevent seizures

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home and is fully dependent on her parents for all activities of daily living and safety supervision; the parents are exhausted from the day's events; the living room has hard-edged coffee tables she keeps colliding with while unsteady

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: severe global developmental delay; completely non-verbal; unable to self-report symptoms

objective_data_offered.value: frequent bursts of unprovoked laughter and vigorous hand-flapping; appears very excitable and agitated
two roughly 30-second spells of going blank and staring while her right arm jerked rhythmically, each followed by going floppy and drowsy
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: witnessed and described by the parents, not clinically observed or recorded

objective_data_offered.value: walking is much more wobbly and wide-based than usual, repeatedly bumping into furniture
does not feel hot and has not had a fever today
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent's impression by touch; no thermometer reading given

objective_data_offered.value: living room has hard-edged coffee tables posing a blunt-trauma risk during her unsteady, agitated phases
